helium_scalar.interpretation: Primordial helium propagates without isotopic fractionation — pure scalar transport. Value ~6.1× atmospheric indicates subduction-modified mantle, not radiogenic crustal decoherence.

carbon_scalar.interpretation: δ¹³C ≈ -5‰ locks to arc magmatic carbon. CO₂/³He ≈ 3×10⁹ matches Japan arc volcanic gases. Scalar density 20× MOR baseline.

methane_decoherence.interpretation: Sediment-derived organic CH₄ introduces directional decoherence into the scalar field. Not inorganic equilibrium (calculated T_eq ≈ 200°C vs actual 320°C).

heat_helium_coupling.interpretation: Matches MOR (3.0×10⁻⁷) and arc magmatic (1.7×10⁻⁷) within one order of magnitude. A planetary coherence constant independent of tectonic setting.

JADE_SITE.notes: Primary magmatic coherence node. Black Smoker chimney acts as scalar aperture — focused emission of mantle volatiles with minimal shallow modification.

helium_scalar.interpretation: Lower R/Rₐ indicates crustal radiogenic ⁴He ingress during secondary circulation through sediment pile. Mantle coherence diluted by shallow source.

carbon_scalar.interpretation: δ¹³C still arc-like, but CO₂/³He >10¹⁰ indicates sedimentary carbon addition. Scalar density reduced by factor ~2.7 vs JADE.

methane_decoherence.interpretation: CH₄/³He ≈ 4×10⁹ — elevated organic contribution relative to JADE. Multiple shallow circulation cells amplify sediment interaction.

heat_helium_coupling.interpretation: Low temperature and secondary circulation disrupt the primary heat-helium scalar coupling.

CLAM_SITE.notes: Secondary decoherence node. Fissure-hosted fluids at ~100°C circulate through porous sedimentary cover. Sulfate reduction by organic decomposition (Gamo et al. 1991) is a decoherence mechanism that converts ordered sulfate into reduced sulfur species, increasing entropy.

scalar_topology.field_gradient: JADE → CLAM represents a scalar density gradient from high coherence (320°C, end-member) to high decoherence (100°C, modified).
scalar_topology.transport_mechanism: Hydrothermal circulation = scalar convection. Helium = ideal scalar tracer (no fractionation). CO₂/³He = conserved scalar ratio. Heat/³He = universal scalar invariant.

boundary_conditions.upper_boundary: Seafloor sediment pile (>1 km) — porous medium where decoherence mechanisms operate
boundary_conditions.lower_boundary: Magmatic heat source / shallow magma chamber — coherence source
boundary_conditions.lateral_boundary: Izena Cauldron wall / Iheya Ridge — structural control on circulation cells

Yokosuka_site.scalar_relevance: Supercritical CO₂ phase separation at Hohenschwangau chimney represents a new coherence regime — scalar density exceeds JADE baseline.

Mo_W_isotopes_2025.scalar_relevance: δ⁹⁸/⁹⁵Mo and δ¹⁸⁶/¹⁸⁴W in hydrothermal fluids reveal redox-controlled scalar fractionation. Mo/W decoupling during phase separation is a decoherence signature.

Boron_isotopes_2025.source: Springer Progress in Earth and Planetary Science 2025
Boron_isotopes_2025.scalar_relevance: δ¹¹B values (-3.3 to +1.5‰) in sediments vs hydrothermal fluids demonstrate pumiceous sediment as a decoherence vector — boron isotope fractionation during fluid-sediment interaction.
